Putting a non-boss figurine in the slot and powering the Materializer with electricity will create a clone of the enemy represented by the figurine.
Encounter gentler monsters in a relaxed, enjoyable setting.

The young animal is very happy and full.

The Abiotic Factor scene now correctly shows any hoed ground as Stone hoed ground (it will no longer show hoed Turf ground on top of Stone ground). This fix will only be applied to worlds where this scene was not generated prior to the fix.
Painted Wood Fences now appear with the correct colors on the map.
Fixed controller vibration not always stopping.

Added an “-activateallcontent” argument for dedicated server to automatically activate all available content bundles. If you include this argument in your startup script, you do not have to activate new content bundles individually when the game receives an update. See ARGUMENTS.txt for more information.
It is now possible to copy a world's seed with a single click in the world settings screen.
Added two new tracks in the Dirt biome: "Mysteries Uncovered" and "Light From Above".
Added Potion Pouch and Critter Pouch to provide dedicated storage for critters and potions.
The scene that contained the Ruins Arc stone slate can once again naturally spawn in the world.
Added 8 Echo-Maps that mark important locations in the world.
New Biome: Breaker's Reach - Found in the far north.
The Locked Solarite Chest can now be found in the waters of the Shimmering Frontier.
New Minion: Mining Minion - Spawn a pickaxe to help you mine.
New mushroom variant: the Glowing Mushroom - Found in the Breaker's Reach Void sub-biome.
New Boss: S.A.H.A.B.A.R. - Found in the Breaker's Reach biome.
Added more than 20 new pieces of decoration.
Added some new scenes to already existing biomes.
Added descriptions to some large destructible objects.
Added 10 new figurines for the new enemies and bosses.
Added 3 new critters found in the Breaker's Reach.
Added 3 new valuables exclusive to the Breaker's Reach.
New Hydra: Oblidra the Void Lord - Found in the Breaker's Reach biome.
Added an Otherworldly Merchant found in the Breaker's Reach. This merchant may sell some very important items!
The Shrooman enemy now has another anticipation sound effect that might play when it's preparing to attack.
All pieces of the Miner set now drop from all sources that dropped them individually before.
Text keys containing ':' are now remapped to '_' when matched to TextDataBlock names.
Circuits, conveyor belts, drills, doors, levers, turrets, and pressure plates now regenerate health over time, matching the behavior of other placeable objects.
The cattle UI no longer incorrectly claims that baby Drohmbledores are producing anything before growing up.
Updated the graphics and the names of Baits (the ones that can be crafted in the Fishing Workbench) to Lures.
Both the player and automated systems now have a base chance for planting golden crops.
Weapons classified as "beam weapons" should now display their aiming direction via a crosshair cursor. The affected weapons are:
Beds can now correctly be painted regardless of if the player is selecting the pillow or the sheets when attempting to paint.
Fixed a race condition that could cause memory to be accessed after it had been deallocated.
Biome-specific terrariums and aquariums are now paintable.
Fixed an issue where the Stone Lantern wouldn't emit any glow if painted with a Black Paint Brush.
Fixed an issue where part of the inventory sort button was not clickable while in Creative mode.
The Glurch Eye, Ghorm's Horn and Stolen Crystal Heart items can now be incinerated.
Fixed an issue where bosses sometimes failed to drop their loot if the player died or left the area during the boss' death animation.
Fixed a bug where bridges would sometimes be invisible after wall blocks placed on top of them were broken.
Burn debuffs no longer always refresh on new applications, but only when they have more total damage than the current remaining burn debuff.
Pressing Cancel on the Join Game screen immediately after starting to join no longer causes future join attempts to timeout.
Has shorter healing and mana potion cooldowns.
Resolved an issue where the Caveling Gardener’s attack pattern did not behave as intended.
Caveling Gardeners now pursue targets at a range that allows them to use their melee attack effectively.
Fixed an issue where some visual effects would not play in the final boss' second phase.
The name and save icon of an existing world can now be changed in its world settings screen.
Chests with emissive textures now glow in the dark.
Folding Screens can now correctly be rotated.
The Nimruza Figurine can no longer be placed in the Materializer.

This hotfix fixes some sources of crashes following the Unity 6 engine upgrade in 1.1.2.6. These fixes are based on player reports, and we do not know for sure if this addresses all crash sources. If you continue experiencing crashes, please get in touch using the community link on the game's main menu.
Clients now receive and process animation triggers from the server in a worker thread job instead of on the main thread.
Closing the map using the “back” button on a gamepad no longer triggers offhand item effects.
Improved how the game finds converters, reducing the game’s load time.
Moving during the Core Commander's death animation no longer causes some particle effects to appear in the wrong location.
Fixed an issue that caused texts in Korean to be garbled.

CraftingAuthoring now uses a ModdedObjectID string when ObjectID is set to ObjectID.None, which will be converted to an ObjectID during runtime. Modders may use this change to add modded items to workbenches and similar items.
It is no longer possible to avoid paying the full crafting cost for parchment recipes by holding some required items in the mouse slot when the crafting animation finishes.
Biome titles are no longer shown while in Creative mode.
Critters no longer clip through walls behind them.
Fixed a bug where the Nuclear Battery would sometimes not drop after defeating an Ancient Battle Arena.
Boulders in the Crystal Meteor scene are no longer invisible.
Crystal Snails no longer destroy drills when moving.
Fixed a lag spike when connecting to a world using a new character.
Player death markers on the map now have the same color as the marker for the player who perished there.
Fixed an issue that caused the wrong player name to be displayed on grave map markers.
Dedicated servers will no longer have a hard crash if Steam fails to initialize. Servers will instead close gracefully with an appropriate log message.
Resolved a bug where Delay Circuits failed to update if they were powered by an Electricity Stick and lost their secondary signal of 2 from another source.
Flower Vessel destructibles in Azeos’ Wilderness now correctly leave flower debris on the ground when destroyed.
Added a feature to detect more startup issues which may be caused by mods. The game will now try to restart without mods if possible.
Fishing in normal water in the Shimmering Frontier or Passage biomes no longer causes the player to get stuck when trying to pull up their catch.
The minimap no longer stays visible in the main menu after getting disconnected from a server.
When playing on the moddebug Steam branch, the game no longer automatically restarts with mods disabled if a mod fails to load.
Steam Achievements will no longer trigger if use of console commands is detected during a game session.
Fixed an issue that caused the Quantum Console to open if a player using a gamepad clicked specific buttons.
In game sessions where Quantum Console is enabled, after it has been opened once, players will only be prompted with a help text pop-up once. This pop-up will not appear in future sessions.
Sprinklers now keep the ground watered even when no players are nearby.
The Dresser's shadow now correctly rotates with the Dresser.
Fixed an issue that caused drills to damage walkable tiles (e.g. Rails, Conveyor Belts).
Fixed an issue that would cause a lag spike every time a new dungeon started generating. This issue was especially noticeable when starting a new world on a device with few cores.
Electrical structures are now attempted to be sent to the client every server tick, reducing visual inconsistencies when powering on or off.
The Electrical UI now features a blinking lightning icon and an improved tooltip.
Fixed a long-standing issue where toggling/destroying electricity sources could cause one tick of zero electricity in connected components, even if connected to a second active source.
Achievements no longer stop working after playing for more than one hour.
Reduced the rarity of the Unleashed Core Commander Figurine from Legendary to Epic, meaning the item can now be deleted if desired.
The Metal Industrial Box and the Reinforced Industrial Box no longer erroneously render in front of Wall Light Bulbs.
Updated a Breaker's Reach puzzle scene to prevent a soft lock. This fix will only be applied to worlds where this scene was not generated prior to the fix.

Updated a Breaker's Reach puzzle scene pedestal to contain a Shrooman Brute Figurine instead of a non-figurine item. This fix will only be applied to worlds where this scene was not generated prior to the fix.
Poison Berry Walls' explosions should now correctly apply the poison effect.
Fixed issues that prevented cloud saves from being accessed or updated.
Fixed issues that prevented players from different storefronts or console generations from playing together.
Fixed issues that prevented the game from starting correctly.
Fixed some issues that were causing the game to crash.
Fixed issues with how the game receives controller input.

This update fixes one or more major issues in the game. To allow us to release it as quickly as possible, we have not translated the detailed changelog into English. Below you will find pre-translated descriptions of the type of issues we have fixed. Please see the English changelog for more details.
Fixed issues with in-game texts or translations.
Fixed missing or incorrect loot drops.
Fixed issues with the game's mod support.
Fixed issues that prevented players from connecting to each other in multiplayer.
Fixed problems with the in-game physics.
Fixed issues that prevented save files from being loaded or written correctly.
Fixed issues with the in-game user interface.
Updated libraries the game depends on.
Fixed issues with the world generation.
Robot Arm filters now correctly apply when extracting items from Fishing Nets and Critter Catchers.
Added 5 Contributor Items. These are items to celebrate the contributions someone made to the game or the community.
Fixed assigning pause button for opening map or inventory causing player to be unable to access pause menu, softlocking themselves.
Boats no longer block object placement on nearby ground.
Fixed Beds sometimes not properly being unclaimed when a player left the game.
Fixed an issue that caused critters in Terrariums to face the wrong direction.
Fixed an issue where squashing critters could cause critters in Terrariums to not display correctly.
Minions and pets no longer target Crystal Snails.
Fixed an issue which made it possible to duplicate the Dynamite Pack and the Remote Explosive Pack.
Fixed an issue that caused Galaxite Turrets to take longer than intended to be destroyed.
Fixed an issue where Nimruza never healed up despite players being far away.
Fixed an issue in Creative worlds where God Mode could not be toggled on.
Fixed an issue that caused a black square to appear around Ground Slime when it was placed on Glass Bridges.
Fixed entities that heal nearby allies sometimes failing to heal all valid targets within range.
Boulders in the Crystal Meteor scene are no longer invisible. For real this time!
Fixed an issue that caused Life on Melee Hit to have no effect.
Fixed an issue where locked item slots remained locked when the item was dropped after the player died.
Mana Potions now have a separate cooldown and no longer share one with other consumables.
Fixed minions and pets being teleported back to the player when near Cocoons.
Fixed an issue where it was not possible to navigate to a Sign's text field with a gamepad.
Fixed several cases where the wrong tooltip would show up when hovering over the network status buttons in the pause menu.
Pasting long texts in input boxes will now paste the part that fits instead of nothing.
Newly hatched pets that have been dropped on the ground are no longer deleted when the world is reloaded.
Fixed an issue where copying a world from one server or host to another might lead to characters having their inventory and other character data reverted when joining the world.
Fixed an issue where the key binding to toggle the shortcut window was not shown.
Fixed an issue where some Linux systems experienced several minutes of stalling during game startup.
Enemies that destroy tiles when they spawn can no longer destroy immune tiles.
Improved texts and fixed some localization issues.
Fixed an exploit where the Titan Breath could be fired infinitely without consuming mana by holding both attack buttons simultaneously.
Fixed an issue where Nimruza spawned too many Cicada Nymphs.
Fixed several issues that caused some characters to not appear correctly in the game in various languages.
Fixed an issue that caused the Desert Guardian Pants to not be included in the Desert of Beginnings fishing loot.
Holding the use button to open seasonal items (such as Winter Presents or Red Envelopes) in quick succession now works correctly once again.
Adjusted the Quill Rifle's projectiles' lifetime.
Fixed an issue where Flamethrower Turrets would fail to damage nearby enemies when the player was too far away.
Fixed an issue where players could still move while being forced to sleep.
Reduced damage and knockback from friendly explosions (weapons and grenades) for players, while remaining unchanged for opposing PvP teams.
The Mana channeling talent now gives flat Mana instead of a percentage.
Items no longer lose durability while the player is in God mode in a Creative world.
Gravestone markers now visually appear over Waypoints and Portals on the map.
Gravestone markers can no longer prevent you from teleporting to a Waypoint or Portal.
The version number in the top right corner is now correctly only shown while the Settings menu is open.
Using a hoe on Wires beneath Conveyor Belts now correctly removes the Conveyor Belt before removing the Wires.
Improved clarity of the titles in the Ancient Hologram Pod.
After a hydra boss has been killed in a world, their scanner and summoning item will now become available in the Ancient Hologram Pod.
The Impersonator Syndrome Achievement can now correctly be achieved.
Fixed an issue where identical combat text from multiple sources in the same frame would only display once.
Improved the names and descriptions of certain items, to better follow conventions and improve clarity.
Increased the health of the Floracada.
Increased the maximum vibration intensity on controller.
Increased the drop chance of the Ninja armor set by 200%.
Increased amount of Mechanical Parts sold by the Cloaked Merchant.
Introduced a new Casual character type that:
Iron Workbench now costs Planks instead of Wood.
The Item Collector no longer plays the second teleportation effect if the item was not dropped due to electricity being interrupted.
Added the ability to label chests.
Fixed lag spikes that would sometimes happen when bosses temporarily went off-screen.
The Large Quarry Timber's position when placed on the ground now correctly matches its visual placement indicator, and the item now shows a rotated sprite when facing upwards.
Warnings about missing localizations in the TextDataBlock editor are now less intrusive and more descriptive.
The Electrical Door is now paintable.
Robot Arms are now capable of filtering items.
Using the Magic Mirror no longer makes the character appear naked to other clients in the same session.
Azeos the Sky Titan Scanner once again requires Ancient Feathers to be crafted.
Malugaz should no longer be able to teleport outside his combat arena.
The map is no longer initially blank when opened at high zoom levels using a gamepad.
Map markers on the minimap no longer have the wrong icon for one frame after using a scanner.
Meadow Milk can now be cooked.
Improved how menus are initialized to reduce the game’s load time.
The Scrap Minigun can no longer be charged up when at 0 durability.
Fixed minion detonators activating when a minion’s lifetime ended, which caused the minions to explode with no player input.
The Minion Kindler should now drop the correct materials when salvaged.
Minions should now be correctly slowed by mold ground.
Fixed an issue that caused minions not to immediately follow a command when told to move to the same location without moving the cursor.
Fixed an issue that caused minions to attempt to attack bosses while they were immune to damage.
Fixed a bug where some Abiotic Factor loot was not dropping.
Added a missing translation for the Multiplayer Connectivity option.
"Fixed a bug where the ""caught fish"" chat message did not appear if you immediately threw the hook back into the water while having a bait equipped."
Fixed a bug where Floracadas would not drop any loot.
Fixed a bug where inventory slots added by pouches would sometimes not show the background icon indicating which item type can be placed there.
Fixed a bug where recipe slots would not highlight at the correct times during the in-game tutorial.

Mods that are automatically installed because they are dependencies of other mods are now marked as dependencies in the mod menus. They also can no longer be uninstalled manually unless you first uninstall all mods that require them as dependencies.
QuantumConsole is now accessible to mod developers and can be switched on by setting MenuManager.enableConsole to true. It can then be opened in-game via CTRL + X.
ModIO mod collection menu now displays mod size in localized “megabytes/kilobytes/bytes” text whenever unsubscribing from a mod.
When creating a new Pooled Mod Graphical Object Bank instance, the default sizes will now be 8 for Initial Size, 8 for Max Free Size and 1024 for Max Size.
Core Keeper now uses ScriptableDataBlocks which are just like ScriptableObjects but hold an address synced to the assets' GUID.
Gradient Maps are now stored as a texture array inside of GradientMapDataBlocks.
Sprite Objects now reference SpriteAssetDataBlocks and GradientMapDataBlocks.
Mods can now be loaded from Steam Workshop as well.
The mouse cursor has been upgraded to use a SpriteAsset, which can now be modded via ScriptableData Editor Window.
Holding an item and closing the inventory while using a gamepad no longer results in a noticeable delay before the character can move again.
Moved the Lit Floor to Carpenter's Table.
Moved the moment when the game checks for full storage to happen later, to allow for deleting worlds when this occurs.
The Table Saw has been moved to the Tin Workbench.
Fixed a crash while quitting the game.
You can now select multiple items at once when trashing items.
Added a new loading animation that is displayed before the logos when the game starts.
Added lots of new scenes to already existing biomes.
Added 1 new item that permanently gives more max health.
Added 1 new Summoning Tome that allows summoning a mining minion.
Burn Resistance Potion: makes you immune to burning for a short while. Bought from Otherworldly Merchant.
Greater Mana Potion: refills your Mana by a bigger amount. Crafted in the Laboratory Table.
Mana Potion: refills your Mana by a small amount. Crafted in the Alchemy Table.
Added 17 new puzzle rooms that can be solved for rewards.
Advanced Automation Table. Crafted in the Octarine Workbench.
Metalcrafter's Table. Bought from Otherworldly Merchant.
Relucite Smithing Table. Bought from Otherworldly Merchant.
The name that appeared above Nimruza's weak spot during the fight will now be translated correctly into all languages.
Fixed an issue that could result in the duplication of the trails spawned by the secondary use of certain weapons.
Fixed an issue that allowed non-player inventory items to be sent to the trash can slot.
Fixed an issue where unmined ore might disappear when loading a world, especially in areas close to dungeons and other structures.
Organized the items in Creative Mode by splitting up categories and moving them into more appropriate locations.
Fixed an issue that caused outlines to be displayed incorrectly during multiplayer sessions.
Fixed a bug where the final outro would not play if the world was in Guest mode with at least one non-admin player.
Fixed several inconsistencies for how pet names were displayed after being placed in a pet bed.
The player's item pickup-radius is now blocked by doors and walls.
Fixed an issue where items would sometimes fail to be automatically pulled toward the player when standing near walls.
Planter Boxes and Decorative Pots are now paintable.
Fixed an issue where the player inventory could be reverted to a previous state when connecting to a world whose save file had been copied from another save slot or machine.
Fixed Cartography Table not sending data in some situations.
Fixed an issue that caused two clients from the same location to not be able to join the same session.
Improved the Cartography Table and fixed some issues related to it.

All Graphical Prefabs which have a component that inherits from IPoolable will now automatically be pooled. In order to adjust the size of pooling or to disable it, modders may create a PooledModGraphicalObjectBank ScriptableObject via the Create menu.
Getting disconnected from a server while a popup dialog is dispayed no longer results in menus still being open in the background when returning to the main menu.
Putting pouches into non-consecutive slots no longer makes it impossible to navigate between them when using a gamepad.
Fixed an issue that would cause some inventory animations to play twice, item pickup animations to not play at all, and UI actions to feel unresponsive.
Projectiles should no longer deal damage for extended periods after they've collided with environment.
The following items can now be picked up with a hoe or shovel:
Fixed an issue where PvP teams would sometimes show the wrong color after toggling PvP on and off.
Ra-Akar's homing projectiles now despawn when the boss is defeated.
Fixed a bug where only the first new character created in a session had their appearance randomized by default.
Reduced the damage caused to tiles by the Scrapzooka (previously called Hand Mortar), in order to better match its intended progression tier.
Recolored older void effects to align with the new void effects.
The Giant Bug Brain no longer drops from Ra-Akar, and is no longer a crafting requirement for Wind Organ of Wilderness (Druidra's summoning item).
Reduced load times when starting the game.
Fixed an issue that caused some sprites to be loaded into memory twice.
The Bait Workbench has been renamed to Fishing Workbench.

Reworded the Crossplay option in the gameplay settings to clarify that this only affects playing with PC players who bought the game on a different storefront. The game currently does not support crossplay between PC and consoles.
Increased the amount of Scarab Wingcovers found in the Desert of Beginnings.
Fixed an issue where scrolling with a controller stick behaved incorrectly at high framerates.
Activating a content bundle using a gamepad no longer results in a hidden "Missing" content bundle being selected.
Suppressed excessive log warnings about host not keeping up with the update frequency target.
Enemy shields now also block explosion damage.
Added a new scene for the Shimmering Merchant in the Shimmering Frontier biome.
Fixed an issue where shockwave attacks from enemies and bosses were not correctly blocked by blocks that were in line of sight.
Crafting tooltips now display the total number of materials available in nearby chests, combined with what is in the player’s inventory.
The Shredder now returns the full amount of Scrap Parts when dismantling items.
Updated the icon shown when chest and sign label visibility is toggled off.
The skill tree selection SFX no longer has significantly lower volume than other UI sounds.
The cookie called "Skip" can no longer be placed in Potion Pouches.
Fixed an issue that caused Bed sleep interactions to not always trigger as intended.
Using a Portal or Waypoint while wearing the Pajama armor set no longer results in an infinite loading screen.

The Pajama armor set now ensures the player has had the set's buff applied for 3 seconds before making the player sleep, rather than only accounting for how long the player has been still regardless of them wearing the set or not.
Slightly decreased the Quill Rifle's physical damage.
Fixed an issue that caused Crystal Snails to not be damaged by drills.
Fixed an issue that caused enemies to spawn too frequently in the Passage.
Improved Urschleim's stability to prevent crashes in certain circumstances.
Fixed a bug that sometimes prevented the Starlight Nautilus achievement from triggering.
Starting a world while not having an internet connection no longer causes the loading screen to stay indefinitely.
Fixed an issue where mods marked with the Script (Elevated Access) tag weren't loading.
Stone Tiles from the Forlorn Metropolis sub-biome now correctly match their inventory loot sprites when placed on the ground.
Fixed a scenario where the game would crash when saving large worlds and maps.
Fixed an issue that caused the accumulated size of save files to cause a crash.
Fixed a bug where save files could become corrupted if a crash happened with bad timing during an auto-save.
Changed some texture resolutions to improve memory usage.
Fixed a potential crash caused by memory being deallocated prematurely when preparing to save the world.
Improved text and translations across multiple languages.
Explosion damage to tiles no longer scales with increased explosion damage effects.

The Core will now guide players to Titan Shrines, which lead the way to the next step in the game's progression. At these ancient structures, players can craft scanners and summoning items, and unlock a waypoint.
These Titan Shrines can be added to worlds created before the 1.2 update through a new Content Bundle, which can be found in the world settings screen.
Hovering Titan Breath's buff icon now displays the correct percentage.
Fixed a scenario where the game would crash, caused by moving items in the inventory after using the Item Locking or Item Removal features.
Fixed a scenario where the game would become unresponsive when moving items in the inventory after using the Item Locking or Item Removal features.
Added 2 new materials found in the Breaker's Reach:
The Amber Boulder scene is now guaranteed to spawn once per world. This only applies to newly created worlds.
Upgraded Unity engine from version 2022.3.50f1 to 2022.3.62f2.
Upgraded Unity engine from version 6000.0.58f2 to 6000.0.59f2. This fixes a bug in the engine that could cause the game to crash from particle system memory becoming corrupted.
Upgraded Unity engine from version 2022.3.62f2 to 6000.0.58f2.
Upgraded Unity Addressables package from version 1.22.3 to 2.7.3.
Upgraded Unity Collections package from version 1.2.4 to 2.5.2.
Upgraded Unity Entities package from version 1.2.4 to 1.3.9.
Upgraded Unity Netcode for Entities package from version 1.2.4 to 1.4.0.
Upgraded Unity Physics package from version 1.2.4 to 1.3.9.
Player equipment textures are now completely unloaded when not in use.
Updated some of the Core's dialogues to better explain the next tasks for the player.
Updated the visuals for some boss attack indicators.
Updated the graphics for Ancient Feathers, Sea Shells and Scarab Wingcovers when found on the ground.
Updated the graphics for the Grimy Bookshelf and the Large Grimy Bookshelf.
Updated some of the icons and names of categories in the Creative mode UI.
Updated the loot sprites for the boss scanners.
Updated sound effects for various items and UI interactions.
Fixed some rare game crashes during the Urschleim boss fight.
Prevented the Oblidra Chest from spawning inside walls.
Improved the way Primeval Slime Cells are used, in order to avoid consuming multiple of them at once.
Successfully consuming the Primeval Slime Cell no longer causes all scannable map markers to become visible.
Fixed an issue where the Wall Light Bulb couldn't be painted with a Teal Paint Brush.
Walls can now be placed on Explorer Proximity Sensor and Enemy Proximity Sensor.
Rune Song's and Crystal Shard Club's secondary use ability now scales correctly with melee damage and skills.
Weapons of the same type will now be displayed with the same sprite size more consistently when dropped or placed inside a pedestal.
Fixed Creative worlds not showing as Creative in the join game menu.
Fixed a shader issue causing The Shimmering Frontier biome to look very bland.
Support for classic worlds will cease to continue and most 1.0 content will not be accessible. It is strongly recommended to start a world using other modes.

Thank you for getting the Explorer's Edition!
Tackle tougher monsters and reap greater rewards.
In a place far away from anywhere else.
You are part of a group of explorers, making your way through the forest.
Feeling a strange presence,* you notice something between the trees.
Drawn to it,* you approach this strange relic and raise a hand towards it.
Upon touching its surface, a blinding light engulfs you.
Contains an indecipherable entity that appears to respond to glass.
This soda tastes like licorice, or something worse.
An ice cold beverage with a kick of flavor. May cause a slight chill.
A highly caffeinated drink. Sure to knock some fatigue off.
A strange soda, from a strange vending machine.
A large box arbitrarily stocked with research items.
IS-0173-B is a cybernetically augmented Pest (IS-0173), a spiky, loaf-shaped juvenile creature. It lunges to pierce prey with its jaws, quills, and its now inadvertent electricity.
A lifelike representation of an Electro-Pest.
A hefty pipe with a gauge on the end for added weight.
Gadgetry labeled "GATE" that suspends a humming entryway. The rims of its rift depict a laboratorial place of origin, which has been eclipsed by a tether to the Core.
A re-engineered bioweapon, discharging quills as deadly bolts.
A specialized lab coat faintly tinged with radiation. It's probably harmless.
Monocular night vision made from various scientific gadgetry.
A pair of pale leggings designed to idle on office chairs, yet perfectly fit for outrunning extradimensional horrors.
A snack dispenser with contents in surprisingly fresh condition.
A jug-based water cooler, great for holding and dispensing liquids.
A whiteboard covered with convoluted, scribbled equations.
A box arbitrarily stocked with research items.
The resting place of a former adventurer?
An abandoned makeshift tent, who would have lived here?
A tree that grows in the chasms of Azeos Wilderness. It is unknown how far down it reaches.
A lifelike representation of an Acid Larva.
A porous smooth stone, some believe it wards off evil spirits.
An advanced workbench for creating complex machines.
An advanced workbench for creating rings and necklaces.
A leatherbound book written in unsteady human language. It appears that the author believed to have found the mythological center of the earth, before succumbing to illness.
Workbench to tinker with the wonders of chemistry.
A technological marvel: an inventory chest from outer space! Seems like even advanced civilizations need to stash things away.
A piece of tech that would still be functional, had it not been ripped out of the floor by greedy hands.
An alien electrical component that appears unstable.
A descriptive stele, carved into mysterious stone. Its meaning is beyond earthly understanding.
A large descriptive stele, carved into mysterious stone. Its meaning is beyond earthly understanding.
It looks like a pillar but its actual function is unknown. Is it a cooling mechanism or does it perhaps store energy?
This airtight jacket keeps the atmosphere around your body evenly pressured and is equipped with a system to increase damage efficiency.
A highly advanced light using a giant ancient gemstone. It almost appears like an oversized torch made for something with even larger hands.
A breakable box that is not from this world. It contains extraterrestrial goods.
A futuristic helm sealed under a big glass dome, leaving the view open to marvel at the miracles of space.
Heavy pants that are less suited for quick movements and more for safely treading on foreign planets.
A piece of alien technology that seems to have burned out.
Solid remains of an ancient forest. The light it reflects is warm and golden.
A perfectly preserved fish egg encapsulated in amber. How those two combined is a mystery.
Grub encapsulated in amber, an extremely rare ingredient.
The petrified shell of an ancient sea creature.
Simple but beautiful necklace featuring a fossil.
This heavy weapon is actually just a ship anchor. Hits very hard and can throw enemies off.
Valuable ancient coin, currency of the underground.
A mysteriously glowing container holding ancient goods.
An unusual feather left by something big.
A very old design for a fishing hook that is not used anymore.
Only those with secret knowledge know where to find this fountain. It is powered by an ammonite fossil that crystalized through the presence of ancient gemstones.
A necklace crafted with only using ancient gemstones as well as a super rare white version.
This shimmering plate has intricate engravings, it looks like a key to something.
This mysterious ring is entirely made out of ancient gemstones, featuring a super rare white one.
This noble robe covers the body in a cascading, silk-like fabric. The woven in ancient gemstone enhance spiritual capabilities.
Strange, blue gemstone emitting waves of energy.
A set of pants that feels very comfortable to wear. Rooting you in the here and now, you can feel your mental focus increasing.
This coin cast in gold seems to be a much older currency than the common ancient coins. It's also worth a whole lot more.
A truly powerful relic of the past, made for fending off the strongest of foes.
A relic that is made in the same way as the Ancient Guardian Necklace, made for fending off the strongest of foes.
A strange device that materializes spirits of the past.
A small wooden box containing all kinds of brushes, pastes and powders that have been used for makeup.
Great architects of the lost civilization must have used this to take down almost any wall in their way.
One of the first weapons ever made by mankind, but this one has an ancient gemstone in place of a normal spearhead.
This moon-blue fish seemed to have had a normal body once, but the water of the Shimmering Frontier corrupted it to be crystalline through and through.
Drenched in blue glaze and stuck with candles, this glow tulip chocolate cake marks a special anniversary.
This blazing blue party hat can be worn to celebrate a special anniversary.
This groovy green party hat can be worn to celebrate a special anniversary.
This perky purple party hat can be worn to celebrate a special anniversary.
This radical red party hat can be worn to celebrate a special anniversary.
An ancient game for two players, there is no rulebook but apparently it was about shifting blocks around.
A plain robe worn by mages in training.
Comfortable and breezy pants that aid in focus.
A sandy tank with the familiar furnishings of the sea.
A glimmering tank full of crystal formations.
A simple tank for all kinds of aquatic creatures.
A rocky tank with plenty of nooks to hide in.
A lush tank full of vibrant plant life.
A gloomy tank full of strange fossils.
A cloak with particular magical patterns woven in.
The magic energies around you let you see just as clearly as with your eyes.
Both sturdy and light, these pants won't get in the way while casting spells.
Unleash a torrent of arcane energies.
A hat that's made to go adventuring. Never falls off, even when a giant boulder is chasing behind.
A good pair of pants that is made for traveling long distances and sneaking past ancient traps.
A leather jacket that's almost too warm to wear on adventures, but it looks cool!
This multi-layered piece of fabric is tightly woven to keep the body cool and protect from coarse, grainy sand.
A mysterious hood for true desert dwellers that keeps the face in shade.
Extremely rare type of jellyfish. It almost feels out of this world.
A large chest resembling the slithering giant in the Sunken Sea. Once having been part of this monstrosity allows it to float on the surface of water.

This is but one of the many hundred hearts of the Atlantean Worm that give it the strength to hurl its gigantic body through the water. Therefore it brims with vitality that you can now call your own.
A fierce looking helm that resembles the head of the Atlantean Worm. How it came to be is a mystery.
This organic necklace looks like a miniature version of the Atlantean Worm. Maybe it is a juvenile that would grow to become as large as its origin.
An organic ring that looks like one of the segments of the Atlantean Worm. It must have grown inside its body as an anomaly.
A dislodged, razorsharp mandible of the Atlantean Worm. The maker of the deep sea.
A lifelike representation of the Atlantean Worm.
Forged from a piece of the sky titan's beak.
A beautiful fan created in honor of Azeos that appears to create wind without swinging it. A gift worthy of a queen.
A colorful talon feather from Azeos, the Sky Titan. By holding it you can dash at a high speed.
A lifelike representation of Azeos the Sky Titan.
A beautiful blue fish with scales that look like feathers.
A small bag holding multiple colored marbles made from glass and cheap gemstones. They have probably been used for child's games.
A pointy lure that can stick to fish from the outside to catch more and catch them faster.
A wiggly lure that looks like a red worm, large enough to allow more than one fish to get caught sometimes.
A well-seasoned type of lure that attracts fish and other things more quickly.
An icky type of lure that may stick on to fish that didn't even bite.
A shimmering lure that catches the eye of more rare fish.
A sugary type of lure that increases the chance of catching fish instead of loot.
A luminous type of lure that increases the chance of pulling up loot.
This lure consists of multiple edible and magical components, attracting the one big catch that lingers in deep waters. Is consumed on successful use.
Quite literally a crystal pillar with bait fixed to its top. Placing it in the waters of the Sunken Sea will make the true terror of the deep aware of your presence if close enough.
A fluffy giant spore. It's non-toxic but also not very useful.
A rag fit for a suspenseful ruffian.
A tall, wooden frame with a sheet of fabric hanging down from the top.
A menacing weapon that swings wide and hits hard.
A place to sleep. Also allows friendly inhabitants to move in if placed in a room to their liking.
A simple fork that has seen too much use.
A giant stone tile decorated with a deep blue glass. It depicts a gathering of cavelings.
A giant stone tile decorated with a deep blue glass. It depicts a glowing gemstone.
A giant stone tile decorated with a deep blue glass. It depicts a robed caveling.
Thrown much like a regular grenade... but creates a stronger blast!
A large table with brass rims and a board made from a sleek alloy.
A giant jungle tree that seems to have grown from the essence of life itself. A must have decoration.
A lifelike representation of a Big Larva.
A lifelike representation of a Big Dracularva.
A blue swim suit piece that keeps everything in its place. Everyone knows the most revealing armor is the most protective.
A green swim suit piece that keeps everything in its place. Everyone knows the most revealing armor is the most protective.
A red swim suit piece that keeps everything in its place. Everyone knows the most revealing armor is the most protective.
A blue underwear piece that goes well with its top part. Get rid of unnecessary clothing and run free in the sun!
A green underwear piece that goes well with its top part. Get rid of unnecessary clothing and run free in the sun!
A red underwear piece that goes well with its top part. Get rid of unnecessary clothing and run free in the sun!
A mysterious string woven from fine filaments. It can be used to bind a set of cards together.
Scans the world for Azeos the Sky Titan. Consumed on successful scan.
A black variant of the bubble pearl, from certain angles it appears golden.
The black variant of a desert diamond that is highly sought after by miners. It seems to invert reflected light and cast it back as shadows.
A necklace made from pitch-black metal that leaves dark smudges on your neck.
A ring made from pitch-black metal, darkened by the constant presence of immense heat.
A rare sea critter with spikes as hard as steel.
A rusty amalgam of plates fit to endure the fiercest demolitions.
A heavy capsule that would be airtight if not for the narrow slits of its faceplate.
A pair of sturdy sabatons impervious to being windswept from shockwaves.
A volatile powder that ignites under pressure, releasing a sharp, unmistakable odor.
A handful of healthy cereal that fills you up and gives you energy.
A very rare variant of the common bloat oat.
Grows into a filling crop when planted.
Sticky necklace infused with energy made for an unknown purpose.
The crimson remains of someone meeting a terrible fate.
A simple weapon loaded with poisonous darts.
Small, blue fish home to the dirt cave lakes.
This flying bug always appears in swarms which appear as dancing lights. Its blue glow is biome-specific.
A delicate instrument that has multiple fractures across its surface, rendering it unusable. There is a signature reading "Woopsiee", that has been carved into the glass.
A piece of deep blue glass, it appears to be a fusion of molten sand and ancient gemstones.
A blue lump of marine plants.
A lifelike representation of a slippery Blue Slime.
Triggers a directional sequence of explosions, perfect for clearing a path.
This wide workstation allows the construction of different boats. Ahoy!
A stronger explosive for crowd control and facing giant enemies.
A necklace made from a fuse tied to a tiny bomb. Hold on to your head if it sizzles right next to your ear.
A fierce vegetable for spicing up foods.
A very rare variant of the common bomb pepper.
Grows into a spicy vegetable when planted.
A ring crowned with a little bomb idol. Handling explosives while wearing it results in an even stronger blast.
A lifelike representation of a Bomb Scarab.
A crude piece of jewelry carved from bone.
A primitive piece of jewelry made from small bones. Early humans have also made these for many different reasons.
A prehistoric, crude ring made from bone. Rings like these have been discovered in early human graves.
A large, ancient chest crested with gemstones.
A bright yellow fish with long barbels that it drags along the sea floor in search for food.
A simple ring that forms a magical veil.
These chocolates come in a nice gift box and are mouth and heart melting.
A bright fish with fiery belly that seems to purely feed from liquid stone and the gemstones caught within.
A broken container made from clay. It was used to carry rations of water across the desert.
A partially broken grave that either survived the currents of time or a quick attempt to gain treasure.
A piece of an ancient weapon. Even though it's only the handle it already feels mysteriously balanced in your hand.
Unstable pillar for decoration, probably thousands of years old.
A leaking wooden ship that is missing its sail.
A good armor to survive battles more easily.
This helm repels slight blows to the head.
This brown, agile fish is able to move between water holes in the desert to change feeding grounds.
A strange ranged weapon that shoots out hovering bubbles, adding slippery movement on contact.
A beautiful, iridescent pearl. It seems to be hollow.
Natural grown jewelry made from rare bubble pearls.
A bucket. The clever solution to transporting liquids.
This handy tool allows you to catch tiny critters instead of accidentally stepping on them.
Dousing yourself with this elixir makes you flame retardant. Fire may still hurt, but it won't linger.
This fiery weapon was modeled after the grubzooka but enhanced with lava rock shielding to contain its power. Can blast away stone walls and anything weaker.
A stubby patch of grass to use as a decorative.
A contorted remnant of what was once a robotic limb.
A small butterfly with deep red wings that seeks the company of other critters.
An erraticly fluttering butterfly that has bright colors irritating to the naked eye.
Said to be a bringer of good dreams this butterfly is always seen among sleeping creatures.
This blue butterfly flaps its wings so fast that it creates cold air streams.
A tiny butterfly with very delicate wings. Catching it without harming them requires a lot of caution.
A composite material of calcite and fossilized seashells.
A useful calendar that will attract a shady merchant when placed in a habitable room. Depending on the season, he will put on different costumes and offer new items.
A young Drohmble, eager to explore the desert.
Evolved to survive the harsh desert climate, it produces a potent chemical base.
Great for huddling together, telling stories and burning marshmallows to a crisp.
This simple decorative candle can be placed anywhere to set up a cozy atmosphere.
A set of candles for your decoration needs or the right atmosphere when summoning evil entities.
A tall candelabrum hosting three candlelike burners. Its esoteric material emits a purple flame.
An organic body armor resembling Ghorm's shell, it is heavy and feels weird on the inside.
A chitin-plated head piece, wearing it fills you with the urge to dig paths through the darkness.
Rock-hard vegetable that toughens you up.
A very rare variant of the common carrock.
A tough seed that only grows in prepared stone.
A table to share your map discoveries with other explorers.
It looks like an Ancient Gemstone but seems to be extremely dense with energy. Has it been used to grow the Shimmering Frontier?
Useful, handy and secure: the box. One that cattle will feel comfortable to be carried around in and delivered right onto the doorstep of your next farm. Can only be used once.
This useful tray can be filled to the brim with cattle feed to keep cattle fed and happy.
A workbench to create helpful tools and furniture for keeping happy animals.
Every underground miner needs one of those!
Odd necklace made from a fish that never let go of the hook.
A lifelike representation of a Caveling Assassin
Dry, hard bread. It feels like chewing chalk but fills the stomach.
Made from grub leather, good for unhindered movement in combat.
A lifelike representation of a Caveling Brute.
A strong, bottled brew made from unknown ingredients. After ingesting it's hard for you to stand still.
A chunky, sweet snack in a wrapper. Must be thousands of years old and probably still edible for a couple more.
This small drinking cup must have held many different beverages. There is still a faint smell of something sweet.
A handmade doll found in a grave. It must once have belonged to a child...
A small depiction of a caveling, it's hollow and must have been used for storing small goods.
A lifelike representation of a Caveling Gardener.
A dark version of the ancient floor tiles, it looks much older.
A crumbly stone slab with simple patterns embedded in it.
Light armor hood that hides the face.
A lifelike representation of a Caveling Hunter.
A sturdy card that once belonged to a citizen of the forlorn metropolis. It's thousand of years old but the excellent print hasn't faded.
A lifelike representation of a Infected Caveling.
A minted medallion with a face relief. Perhaps it was once awarded to an extraordinary individual?
An old crystal ring, holding it fills you with a sad feeling you can't describe.
A lifelike representation of a Caveling Mummy.

A gooey, constantly dripping helm that covers half of your face.

This staff is drenched in slime that never seems to end no matter how much is flung away.
A strange weapon, good for damaging enemies and slowing them down.
A lifelike representation of a common Orange Slime.
Simple ranged weapon to fire away at enemies.
Scans the world for Morpha the Aquatic Mass. Consumed on successful scan.
A blue slime blob almost too slick to hold onto. Can be further processed.
A blue variant of the slime sword. It sometimes spreads slime onto your enemies that makes them slide around.
A small group of long-necked barnacles stretching out from the ground.
A small explosive to blast walls and enemies.
A caveling skull, smaller than usual. It hints at a tragic story.
A strange growth that consists of broad, transparent leaves.
Not a tree at all, but an invertebrate feeding on floating particles with its arms. It hasn't fully matured yet.
Not a tree at all, but an invertebrate feeding on floating particles with its arms. It hasn't fully matured yet.
A table with brass rims and a board made from a sleek alloy.
A weak light to attach to your belt.
This small crystal formation pops and sputters with chaotic energy.
A small, ancient rug caked with the dust of thousands of years. The design on it shows a trinity of eyes.
A kiln that can melt valuable ores.
An old device used to circulate air into a forge to create extreme temperatures. The lever for its mechanism is rusted in place.
Reinforced with several rare materials, this glove can withstand great heat and is a must have for every experienced blacksmith.
Scans the world for the Atlantean Worm. Consumed on successful scan.
A lifelike representation of a Snare Plant.
This little globe has the core as its center and starts a snowfall when shaken.
A handy projectile to start some fights with the neighbours' children. Can also be collected and further processed.
A traditional festive contruct built with snowballs and a light source in the middle.
Whatever one is feeling at the moment, this hat covers it all up with a happy expression made from coal and carrots.
Comfy and lightweight, this poncho flows well as you run.
A stylish hat and scarf, fit for one another.
Comes equipped with winged boots that should aid your mobility.
A soft, squishy sponge. Time to take a bath!
A superior anvil for creating magnificent equipment.
A magnificent ingot for masterful blacksmithing.
A shimmering chest made from solarite. It is not locked anymore.
A grand glimmering crossbow that shoots mighty bolts.
A superior tool for pulling up elusive fish.
A shimmering key cast in solarite. There should be a lock somewhere that it fits into.
A superior tool for breaking down perplexingly hard walls.
An ore that fused with Ancient Gemstones. It can sometimes be found in crystalline walls.
A superior weapon for obliterating foes.
A superior workbench to be prepared for dire trials.
A very defensive fish with rock-hard skin and sharp spikes protruding out.
A robust robe, using magic fused into the fabric to withstand attacks.
A hood worn by Initiated mages.
Sturdy pants with plenty of pockets for magical trinkets.
A vessel with a hot, oil-like substance within.
A depressing stone amulet. You hear whispering...
A magnificent gemstone that glitters in pastel rainbow colors.
Emits a sharp alert when triggered, warning anyone nearby that something is about to happen.
A crudely-made rack that is used by cavelings to have their spears at the ready.
Allows traveling over water much faster.
A racing kart with a nice red finish, able to outrace other vehicles after a longer acceleration time. Tends to drift a lot when driving too fast.
A mechanical spike trap that only reacts to enemies.
A ring found at the skeleton of a giant ocean creature. It's made from tiny cervical vertebrae.
A ghastly fish that floats in the air like it's still underwater.
It looks almost too scary to remove its wrapper. What tasty horrors might await behind?
A useful gadget that waters tilled ground in a small radius.
A stone pillar grown over thousands of years through dripping water.
A mysterious living fossil that seems to have fused with precious metals and gemstones to ascend to a higher state of being.
A collection of sparkling colorful crystals. If they grew natural or not remains a mystery.
Used to cross chasms and water but also makes for a nice floor.
Rock-hard mushrooms attached to a stone slate. They look edible but are actually fossilized.
Ground plants found in the Forgotten Ruins. They grow tiny berries that attract cavelings of the corresponding area.
A sturdier door to block out unwanted creatures.
A more reinforced fence to protect what's rightfully yours.
A simple gate for the stone fence.
Simple rock tiles for decorating your base.
An unknown light source sits inside this solid stone cage. The air around it feels cold.
A handheld cannon hurling rocks into the air. They won't know what hit 'em!
A useful tool that makes planting seeds a breeze. Covers an area of up to 2x2 tiles.
A stone pillar slowly raised over thousands of years through tectonic pressure.
A salty elixir that hardens your skin and makes you more resilient.
Creates a blast that overwhelms the senses.
The elegant jacket has a stuck-on rose and is perfect for a romantic dinner. Remember to show up on time.
This hat is worn by everyone who considers themself an admirable adventurer equipped with a captivating charisma.
These pants are made from black, silky fabric and hint at sophistication and confidence.
A large bomb harnessing the power of pressurized toxic gas.
This volatile mass latches its plating onto your body. It feels ready to retaliate.
A sapling grows inside this fragile bulb of toxic gas. The lightest touch can make it burst!
Like a crown, when worn, you assert yourself dominant over the forces within this sulfuric mass.
The gel padding adds a bounce to your step. Surprisingly quiet for the force contained within.
This gnarled tree feasts on the organic matter of the passage. It sprouts glowing sacs of toxic gas.
A lifelike representation of a Sulfur Worm.
The claw marks in this rock reveal dormant energies inside, waiting to be awoken.
Used by great mages to conjure small stars.
Nothing is more important than sporting cool sunglasses, even when it's dark.
A vibrant crop that braces you for tough encounters.
A very rare variant of the common sunrice.
Grows into a bracing crop when planted. Only grows in prepared crystal ground.
A large group of two coral species that grow together to form a symbiosis.
A group of two coral species that grow together to form a symbiosis.
A small group of two coral species that grow together to form a symbiosis.
A large branchy coral that provides protection and food for small sea critters.
A small branchy coral that provides protection and food for small sea critters.
A large coral that develops a wide brim above the water surface, providing a safe island for small organisms.
A small coral that develops a wide brim above the water surface, providing a safe island for small organisms.
A fragile butterfly that has been completely corrupted by crystals.
A light shirt with a lily pad strapped over. A little flower is keeping it tied in place.
This lily pad appears unusually sturdy. It fits on your head nicely.
A light pair of shorts with a lily pad strapped around the hip.
A mysterious feather that allows you to dash out of danger.
Wearing this winged ring creates a slight wind that pushes you forward.
Wearing those chill blue shorts tempts you to constantly form the shaka sign with your hand.
Wearing those chill green shorts tempts you to constantly form the shaka sign with your hand.
Wearing those chill red shorts tempts you to constantly form the shaka sign with your hand.
A circuit that takes directs electricity from 1 direction into 2 other directions.
A simple saw to cut materials into slates. Requires electricity.
A large group of long-necked barnacles stretching out from the ground.
A tall, strange growth that consists of broad, transparent leaves.
A large patch of tall grass to use as a decorative.
A large type of algae that can grow outside of water.
This wooden chest-piece is wrapped in a bundle of mysteriously unwithering leaves.
A wooden mask with decorative leaves and horns attuned to the wildlife.
An animalistic emblem decorated with golden trinkets that ring out to the wild.
Leafy legwear made from ancient wooden roots that seem to connect with the earth whenever one stands still.
A wooden ring with two smaller rings around it. One symbolizes the warden and the other the bonded beast.
A sheet of fabric that can be hung on walls.
A greenish-blue, odd-looking fish with weirdly shaped fins.
This decorative vase has been made in honor of Azeos, the Sky Titan.
A block made from hazy blue glass with golden etchings. The information written on it is lost forever as it has been smashed on purpose.
A block made from hazy blue glass with golden etchings. It seems like these were used as memorials or to mark places of importance.
An ancient sandstone wall with an angled top and covered in indecipherable glyphs. What stories do they tell?
An ancient sandstone wall covered in indecipherable glyphs. What stories do they tell?
This intimidating statue depicts an ancient guardian in exotic garment. Its kneeling pose hints at being in service of a king.
A statue wilfully destroyed by grave thiefs. It has been partially relieved of the green daggers in its stony clasp.
This decorative vase has been made in honor of Omoroth, the Sea Titan.
An ancient golden stand that seems to have held royal belongings once.
A very beautiful, decorative pillar only found in ancient desert temples.
A load-bearing pillar which holds up the foundation of the ancient desert temples.
This decorative vase has been made in honor of Ra-Akar, the Sand Titan.
A golden throne reserved only for the buttocks of ancient royalty.
This ancient trap shoots projectiles at anyone walking into its line of sight. Its inner workings are concealed by a caveling grimace.
A dried piece of tentacle. It's quite tough and keeps your teeth busy.
A lifelike representation of a Tentacle.
An elastic tentacle that lashes out at enemies from afar. It's still alive and wiggling in your hand.
The mutated offspring of its extinct ancestor. Its brown shell had adapted to withstand extreme pressure.
A humid enclosure with sandy terrain.
A cool enclosure full of glistening crystals.
A simple enclosure for all kinds of crawling and fluttering critters.
A warm enclosure with dry, rocky terrain.
A temperate enclosure full of bright budding flowers.
A cold enclosure for the creepiest of critters.
A thin wall made from glass, perfect to create enclosures.
A brambly variant of the common root wood.
A set of curved blades. They're evenly balanced so that throwing them is as easy as pointing a finger.
A strange apparatus that sends shockwaves deep into the ground. Using it at the right spot may awaken something from its slumber. Is consumed on successful use.
A fine filament that has microscopic pieces of ancient gemstones woven into it. Its existence is proof of the absolute mastery over materials by whoever created it.
An empty grave that has been moved out of its alignment in search for valuables.
An intact hourglass, filled with blue sand. It is not clear what period of time it measures, as the sand seems to fall incredibly slowly.
A better anvil for creating gear and weapons more suited for survival.
A slower but more damaging weapon against multiple enemies.
A tough metal ingot for blacksmithing.
A good armor to survive battles more easily.
A small blade that allows for quick stabs.
A durable tool for pulling up bigger fish.
This helm repels slight blows to the head.
A durable tool to prepare ground for farming. Covers an area of up to 3x3 tiles.
A durable tool, good for breaking down walls more easily.
A durable shovel, well-suited for digging up ground. Covers an area of up to 2x2 tiles.
A bulky weapon for smashing foes and walls.
A sharp weapon for slaying foes.
A better workbench to prepare you for the underground world.
Reeks of sulfur and is hot to the touch.
A vile treatise on the conjuring of restless spirits.
More of an unfinished journal detailing the author's obsession with unearthing secrets.
A tome that contains powerful incantions barely utterable by the human tongue.
A mystical tome germinating with verdant potential.
Flipping through its pages causes your fingers to go numb.
More of a pamphlet, beginners guide to shadowmancy.
A precious little ring with a yellow gemstone on top.
A piece of jewelry for the neck that's merely a hoop with a gap. Worn by many different cultures throughout world history.
It lights up your surroundings. Can also be placed on the ground and attached to walls.
An extremely rare enigma of sea life that seems to unify all other fish beneath it.
A towel is about the most massively useful thing an underground miner can have.
A necklace featuring a coiling shell. Its ever turning spiral structure is captivating.
A coat with deep pockets fit for a hoggish, dodgy fancier.
A strange gold piece vibrating with energy.

A symbiosis of an ancient gemstone with organic matter, resulting in a physically impossible material. It pulsates in energetic rhythms of three, a celestial pattern that must have been of utter importance to a spacefaring species.
The petrified remains of a species that still exists to this day, even after 220 million years.
A young that comes up from the ground once it had enough time to slumber in the dark. Its shell is still blank but in time it will be populated by the kelp that it feeds on.

A marine reptile and natural gardener, that chews down on kelp when it grows too plenty. It doesn't need many nutrients, so whatever it eats comes out again in a condensed ball shape that is free for the taking.
A hard shell that once belonged to a graceful sea creature. You feel much more protected when holding it.
A green gem shaped like a spiral.
Powers wires and objects with electricity and is beneath floor-level.
A lifelike representation of the Unleashed Core Commander.
An intricate, unique chest with a purple gemstone locked in its center.
An intricate, unique chest with an orange gemstone locked in its center.
An intricate, unique chest with a green gemstone locked in its center.
A two-component potion that is risky to drink.
A high-tech workbench to upgrade tools and weapons, making them stronger and more effective.
A handful of small, pink candles that fill the air with a whiff of vanille.
This heart-themed chest is great to tuck away little trinkets of love.
This little paper bag contains enough rose petals to cover a 1x1 floor tile with them. Great to lay out a path of love.
A scented letter that contains words of love and appreciation. Give it to someone in dire need of affection.
A golden necklace featuring a big, shiny heart. You feel something you can't describe, when wearing it so close to your own.
A simple painting showing nothing more than a heart. Sometimes this is all one needs in these dark times.
A big plushie, resembling a caveling brute holding a heart. Even those need a little bit of love sometimes.
This rose and silver-colored gift is packed with a variety of different charming items.
A golden ring featuring a candy-colored heart. The perfect gift to give to someone you like.
Nothing speaks "love" like a big fuzzy heart on the floor. It smells like wool and lavender.
An assortment of hearts cut from paper, they look great on walls and make a base just a little bit more lovely.
A workbench containing tools and blueprints to create some beautiful decorative items.
A beeping tablet that won't respond to your inputs. Someone else might find use for it.
A geobot's discarded limb, somehow in excellent condition. It has just enough charge left to curl and splay on its own.
A small totem with gilded details in the likeness of a prophet. To some its price would be too sacred to place. To others it is high.
A small pouch made exclusively for valuables, with dedicated slots that auto-sort items as you gather them.
A black satin cape fit for a vampire. The crimson flip-up collar adds to the evil look.
A bloodsucking fish latching onto everything that has a pulse.
A broken automaton that once contained snacks and other goods. It looks like it was forced open.
An old, still functioning automaton that is proof of advanced technology and a sophisticated society.
This draconic fish has green scales that can withstand remarkably high temperatures.
The unsettling gaze of this ring causes minions to stay on high alert.
An alien workstation for ingenious gear creation.
Draws in nearby enemies before spitting them out in pieces.
This gravity-defying liquid reacts to the air once poured out. It dissolves the veil between this realm and the uncharted (but most certainly inhabited) reaches of existence.
A lifelike representation of a Void Caveling Brute.
A lifelike representation of a Void Caveling.
A lifelike representation of a Void Caveling Shaman.
This club feels light when held, yet heavy when swung.
A heavy contraption of a chestpiece consisting of rusty layers welded to a glasslike dark material.
A helmet brimming with dark pressure. You feel it could burst from the slightest crack.
A pair of rusty sheets coiled around sleek dark purplish leggings.
This still beating heart of the Void Lord pulses inward rather than outward. It brims with vitality that you can now call your own.
A lifelike representation of a Void Larva Cocoon.
A lifelike representation of a Void Larva.
A thorny statue of alien-looking make. Place it in a habitable room and an otherworldly visitor may move in.

A glowing cell that phases through itself, twisted and pulled as if tethered to an unseen presence. Born of Nimruza's parasite, fused with Core-linked tissue, it carries a warped signal that may unseal what even the Core Network could not contain.

A glowing cell that phases through itself, twisted and pulled as if tethered to an unseen presence. Once part of S.A.H.A.B.A.R.'s containment core, it now transmits a distorted echo of the same signal.
A particle weapon with unknown inner workings. It emits balls of void that distort spacetime to inflict high damage.
A mysterious intricate piece of architecture.
A large chest, made from the hardened mass of the gargantuan amoeba. You feel uneasy in its presence.
Scans the world for Urschleim. Consumed on successful scan.
A highly mutative, crystalized cell. To resummon Urschleim, activate it in the Passage where ancient lifeforms still roam. Consumed in the process.
A sturdy piece of city architecture, decorated with glass and gold.
A red, wet piece of clay.
A blue, glistening piece of crystal.
A sturdy dark piece of stone.
A sandy piece of desert stone.
A molten wall that detonates violently, unleashing a massive wave of destructive force across a wide area.
An extremely durable block used to build ancient mazes.
A sturdy piece of ancient architecture, decorated with tiles and gold.
A brown solid piece of dirt.
A block reinforced with support beams, erected to last.
An indestructible piece of silicate from the earth's mantel. You're not supposed to have this…
A sleek wall of robust metal. It has a faint texture of rust.
A tough tuff block. Lesser explorers would need machines to mine this.
A sparse yet firm composite of tuff debris. It seems to be held together more by a purplish, gaseous adhesive than any structural rigidity.
A highly dangerous box of explosives that can be placed like a wall. Don't hit it or it will blow up right in your face.
A treacherous wall packed with poison berries, bursting in a toxic cross when touched.
An otherwordly wall speckled with glittering stars.
Measures the thickness of the wall behind it, though its hand fluctuates.
A solid wall made from glass. Works well for building a base or enclosures.
An enigmatic wall reinforced with glimmering plates.
A lush piece of vertical plants.
A lumpy… living piece of hive material.
The sophisticated alternative to decorative torches.
A sandy piece of the coastal stone.
A mellow piece of earth with little summer flowers growing on it.
Haphazard sheets bolted with screws used for a last-ditch-effort of patchwork.
An unstable, explosive piece of wall.
A tougher block hardened by the scorching winds of the desert.
An indestructible piece of obsidian. You're not supposed to have this…
A bleak stone wall ready to be turned lively with colors.
Layers upon layers of living matter have been crushed into a dense, twisted mass.
A yellow fragile piece of sand.
A reinforced metal wall to set up your base for tough encounters.
A white cold piece of snow.
A solid wall made with interlocking blocks.
A reinforced straw wall to set up a cozy farm.
Measures heat... while in doing so emitting its own.
A soft mossy piece of turf.
A solid wall made from wood. Works well for building a base.
A container used for storing water and filling up watering cans.
Used to water seeds and soil.
A large seaweed that lives off of the light cast down into the wilderness.
A floating plant that is part of a rich aquatic ecosystem.
Has a sweet smelling pollen irresistible to insects.
An ancient stone well to take water from.
A miraculous gemstone device used to teleport to other waypoints and portals. When placed, it takes 10 minutes to charge up before it can be accessed.
A simple, white dress elevating the beauty of its wearer. There are many occasions to wear it but the most important one is being a bride.
A beautiful crown made from leaves and pink roses. It resembles a love that feels like eternal spring.
A broken wheelbarrow stuck in its own molten contents. It seems like it couldn't withstand the extreme heat.
A silky white blossom that fell from a beautiful tree. In full bloom it has a faint almond fragrance and reminds one of the joys of life.
This beautiful white tree is a traditional symbol of the joys of life. It is said that in spring a fairy maiden descends to awake the sleeping cherry trees with her breath.
A small white amphibian with outward-protruding gills that look like corals. It also uses them to draw nutrients out of the waters of the Sunken Sea.
This ring is made from a milky, translucent material. The light it reflects makes it harder for enemies to hit you.
This beautiful bouquet made from white roses will elevate the look of every room. Give it to someone you like dearly.
A rare artifact made from precious, white stone. It doesn't produce a sound when you try to use it.
This golden and white bell produces a fine ringing sound, it's faint and reminds one of falling snow.
Nicely decorated this handful of cookies will put one in a seasonal mood. There's even a little core-shaped one in there.
A full meal to warm your belly in the cold season. Prepared with a roasted larva, hot heartberry sauce, dumplings and some greens.
A worn vest with a simple shirt underneath.
A rustic top hat with various trinkets along the brim.
A pair of jeans with a golden chain strapped on.
A crude bow to shoot foes from afar.
Very crude armor for the upper body. Offers minor protection.
Used to cross chasms and water but also makes for a nice floor.
A small wooden crossbow that swiftly shoots bolts.
A piece of giant root to create all sorts of things.
Close up your base with this simple door.
A simple fence to protect what's rightfully yours.
A simple gate for the wooden fence.
A crude tool for pulling up fish.
Simple wooden slats for decorating your base.
Slight protection for the head but you should look for better options soon.
A crude tool to prepare ground for farming.
A crude tool, useful for breaking down walls.
The next best thing when it comes to lower body armor.
This decorative, wooden pillar is just the basic design for a whole lot more colorful variants.
A useful off-hand item to block hits from enemies.
A crude shovel, useful for digging up ground.
A crude spike trap that only reacts to enemies.
A simple, workable stool made from wood. A true hobbyist might turn this into something more beautiful.
A crude weapon to fend off foes.
A simple, workable table made from wood. A true hobbyist might turn this into something more beautiful.
A wooden box used by someone to store random goods.
A simple wooden ring, nothing more, nothing less.
Driftwood with patterns and carvings showing the Core.
Driftwood with patterns and carvings showing a Glow Tulip.
A circular, brambly root with sharp thorns pointing outward.
The most basic of all workbenches.
An incredibly soft tuft of wool that has a pleasant scent to it.
Cast from thick metal this tool seemed to be attached to a chain. Has it been used to keep the unlawful from running away?
Groundcover made from a net of firm plants.
A reinforced floor, woven from meadow straw.
A fish with large yellow bubbles on its head.
This flying bug always appears in swarms which appear as dancing lights. Its yellow glow is biome-specific.
A yellow lump of marine plants.
A brilliant sword that channels mana into the blade to increase its power.
